<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_chebfun_examples.py. -->
<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/approx/GammaFun.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>The gamma function and its poles</h1>
<h2>Nick Hale, December 2009 in <a href='../'>approx</a><span><a href='/examples/approx/GammaFun.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/approx/GammaFun.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<p>[revised June 2019 by Nick Trefethen]</p>
<p>This example displays some of Chebfun's capabilities for unbounded functions by exploring the gamma function $\Gamma(x)$ on the interval $[-4,4]$.</p>
<p>The gamma function has simple poles at the negative integers and zero. Chebfun can determine the locations and orders of these poles if it is called with the <code>'blowup'</code> and <code>'splitting'</code> flags on. The <code>'exponents'</code> field of the output indicates that each pole is simple, that is, it has a singularity of type $x^{-1}$.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">gam = chebfun('gamma(x)',[-4 4],'blowup','on','splitting','on')
plot(gam), grid on
title('Gamma function')</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">gam =
   chebfun column (5 smooth pieces)
       interval       length     endpoint values   endpoint exponents
[      -4,      -3]       20       Inf      Inf         [-1      -1]  
[      -3,      -2]       25      -Inf     -Inf         [-1      -1]  
[      -2,      -1]       24       Inf      Inf         [-1      -1]  
[      -1,-2.2e-308]       20      -Inf     -Inf         [-1      -1]  
[-2.2e-308,       4]       35       Inf        6         [-1      0]  
vertical scale = Inf    Total length = 124
</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/GammaFun_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>Alternatively, and always a better idea when the information is available, one can instruct Chebfun what poles to put where:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">gam = chebfun('gamma(x)',[-4 -3 -2 -1 0 4],'exps',[-1 -1 -1 -1 -1 0])
plot(gam), grid on
title('Gamma function again')</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">gam =
   chebfun column (5 smooth pieces)
       interval       length     endpoint values   endpoint exponents
[      -4,      -3]       20       Inf      Inf         [-1      -1]  
[      -3,      -2]       25      -Inf     -Inf         [-1      -1]  
[      -2,      -1]       24       Inf      Inf         [-1      -1]  
[      -1,       0]       20      -Inf     -Inf         [-1      -1]  
[       0,       4]       35       Inf        6         [-1      0]  
vertical scale = Inf    Total length = 124
</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/GammaFun_02.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>We can now treat $\Gamma(x)$ like any other chebfun. For example, we can:</p>
<p>(1) Find its reciprocal $1/\Gamma(x)$:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">gam_i = 1/gam;</pre>

<p>(2) Compute the square root $|\Gamma(x)|^{1/2}$:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">absgam = abs(gam);
sqrtgam = real(sqrt(absgam));</pre>

<p>(3) Plot these functions:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot([gam gam_i sqrtgam]), grid on
legend('\Gamma(x)', '1/\Gamma(x)', 'sqrt(|\Gamma(x)|)',...
   'location','southeast')
title('Various related functions')</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/GammaFun_03.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>(4) Plot the critical points:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">hold on
[y r] = minandmax(gam,'local');
[yi ri] = minandmax(gam_i,'local');
[ys rs] = minandmax(sqrtgam,'local');

plot(r,gam(r),'.k',ri,gam_i(ri),'.k', ...
    rs,sqrtgam(rs),'.k','markersize',10), hold off
title('Critical points')</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/GammaFun_04.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>(5) Compute some integrals:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">sum(gam)
sum(absgam)
sum(sqrtgam)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">ans =
   NaN
ans =
   Inf
ans =
  14.043323986892393
</pre>

<p>Do you understand why these results come out not-a-number, infinite, and finite?</p></div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>

  </body>
</div>
